name: HMS Urtica (P 83)
events: The history of HMS Urtica as compiled on this page is extracted from the logbooks of this submarine.
This page was last updated in April 2019.
19 Jun 1944 Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted trials off Barrow following which she departed for Holy Loch. (1)
20 Jun 1944 Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted tials in the Clyde area. On completion she proceeded to Holy Loch where she was placed into full commission. (1)
23 Jun 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (1)
24 Jun 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (1)
25 Jun 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (1)
29 Jun 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. Upon completion of these she proceeded to Campbeltown. (1)
30 Jun 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) shifted from Campbeltown to Holy Loch. (1)
1 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted trials in Loch Long. (2)
2 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) is docked in AFD 7 at Holy Loch. (3)
3 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) is undocked. (3)
4 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) shifted from Holy Loch to Arrochar. (2)
5 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted torpedo discharge trials off Arrochar. (2)
6 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted torpedo discharge trials off Arrochar. (2)
7 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) shifted from Arrochar to Holy Loch. (2)

8 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which served HMS Bridgewater (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN) as the target. These included night exercises. (2)
9 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) returned to Holy Loch on completion of last night's exercises. (2)
10 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted noise trials in Loch Goil. (2)
11 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which served HMS Sardonyx (A/Lt.Cdr. T.A. Easton, RNVR) as the target. (2)
12 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted night attack exercises in the Clyde area. She proceeded to Holy Loch on completion of these. (2)
13 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted speed trials on the measured mile in Loch Long. (2)
14 Jul 1944 HMS Upshot (Lt. H.W. Wilkinson, RN) and HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) both conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which they served as targets for each other. (4)
15 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted attack exercises off Campbeltown during which served HrMs O 9 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) as the target. (2)
16 Jul 1944 HrMs O 9 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) and HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises off Campbeltown with each other. (5)
17 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted night A/S exercises off Campbeltown with aircraft. (2)
18 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (2)
20 Jul 1944 HMS H 50 (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) and HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises off Campbeltown with each other. HMS Urtica also conducted A/S exercises with aircraft. (6)
21 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. These included night exercises. (2)
22 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted attack exercises off Campbeltown during which served HMS H 44 (Lt. R.C. Bucknall, RN) as the target. (2)
25 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd. H. Buckle, RN). (2)
26 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd. H. Buckle, RN). (2)
26 Jul 1944 HMS H 50 (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Bedlington (Skr. A.W. Peak, RNR).
Upon completion of these A/S exercises, attack exercises were carried out with HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN). (7)
28 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (2)
29 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) shifted from Campbeltown to Holy Loch. (2)
31 Jul 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted noise trials in Loch Goil. (2)
1 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. Upon completion of these she proceeded to Campbeltown. (8)
2 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) shifted from Campbeltown to Larne. (8)
3 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted attack exercises off Larne during which HMS Philante (Cdr. L.F. Durnford-Slater, RN) served as the target. (8)
4 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted gunnery exercises off Larne. (8)
6 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises off Larne. (8)
7 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises off Larne. (8)
9 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises off Larne. (8)
10 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises off Larne. (8)
12 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises off Larne. (8)
14 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Larne with HMS Moorsom (Lt.Cdr. J.P. Stewart, DSC, RD, RNR). (8)
15 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises off Larne. (8)
16 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted exercises off Larne. (8)

17 Aug 1944 HMS Tiptoe (Lt.Cdr. P.R.H. Harrison, DSO, DSC and Bar, RN) and HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) both shifted from Larne to Holy Loch. They were escorted by HMS ML 249 (T/Lt. H.W. Evans, RNVR). (9)

19 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which HMS Bridgewater (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN) and HMS Sardonyx (A/Lt.Cdr. T.A. Easton, RNVR) served as the targets. (8)
20 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) returned to Holy Loch on completion of last night's exercises. (8)

21 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which HMS Sardonyx (A/Lt.Cdr. T.A. Easton, RNVR) served as the target. These included night exercises. (8)
22 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) returned to Holy Loch on completion of last night's exercises. (8)
31 Aug 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. K.H. Martin, RN) conducted noise trials in Loch Goil. (8)

3 Oct 1944 HMS Urtica (Lt. R.M. Seaburne-May, DSC, RN) conducted special torpedo trials off Arrochar. (11)

27 May 1945 HMS Urtica (Lt. R.C. Bucknall, RN) arrived at Rothesay. (19)
21 Jun 1945 Around 1115 B/21, HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) and HMS Urtica (Lt. R.C. Bucknall, RN) departed Rothesay for Portsmouth. (20)
25 Jun 1945 Around 0915 B/25, HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) and HMS Urtica (Lt. R.C. Bucknall, RN) arrived at Portsmouth from Rothesay. (20)
29 Aug 1945 HMS Uproar, HMS Unrivalled, HMS Vitality and HMS Urtica departed Portsmouth for Londonderry (Lough Foyle).
1 Sep 1945 HMS Uproar, HMS Unrivalled, HMS Vitality and HMS Urtica arrived at Londonderry (Lough Foyle) from Portsmouth.
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
